title: 8 Herbal Tinctures For Chills
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures can be a great way to relieve chills and boost your immune system. When we're feeling under the weather, a warm cup of herbal tea can be just what we need to help our body recover. But why do herbal teas work so well? The main reason is that they contain powerful antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help fight off infections and reduce fever.  One example of an herb that's often used to combat chills is Echinacea purpurea. This plant is known for its ability to stimulate the immune system and reduce inflammation. Drinking an Echinacea tea can help your body fight off a cold or flu more effectively. Another herb that's great for relieving chills is Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger. Ginger has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce fever and ease a sore throat. Ginkgo biloba is also worth mentioning - this herb is known for its ability to improve blood flow and reduce swelling, which can help your body recover from a cold or flu more quickly.  Using herbal tinctures to relieve chills can bring many benefits to your life. For one, they can help you recover from an illness more quickly, which means less time spent feeling miserable. They can also help prevent illnesses from developing in the first place, by keeping your immune system strong. And, because herbal tinctures are often made from natural ingredients, they can be a more gentle and effective alternative to over-the-counter medications.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea tinctures contains alkylamides, caffeic acid derivatives, and glycosides as its bioactive constituents. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and fight off infections that cause chills. The alkylamides in Echinacea purpurea also stimulate the immune system, increasing the production of white blood cells to combat invading pathogens. Additionally, the glycosides in the tincture have antiviral properties that help combat viral infections that can cause chills and fever. By reducing inflammation and fighting off infections, Echinacea purpurea tinctures can help alleviate chills and promote overall well-being.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal tinctures for chills
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Echinacea purpurea flowers and leaves.
Use a glass jar and fill it with 1 cup of the herb mixture. Add 2 cups of 80% vodka.
Close the jar and shake it well for 2-3 minutes. Let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2 weeks.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass jar. Discard the solids.
Store the tincture in a dark glass bottle with a dropper lid. Take 20-30 drops, 2-3 times a day as needed.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale tinctures contains a compound called gingerol, which is a key bioactive constituent that helps with chills. Gingerol has warming properties that help increase blood flow to the skin, making you feel warmer. This is due to its ability to dilate blood vessels and stimulate the body's natural heat production. Zingiber officinale tinctures also contain shogaol, a bioactive compound that has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce the discomfort associated with chills. When taken, these compounds can help alleviate chills by warming the body and reducing inflammation.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal tinctures for chills
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Zingiber officinale (ginger) roots.
Chop 1/2 cup of the ginger roots into small pieces.
Combine the chopped ginger with 2 cups of 80% vodka in a clean glass jar.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2 weeks. Shake the jar every day.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle. Discard the solids and store the tincture in a cool, dark place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba tinctures contains flavonoids and terpene lactones, which help to combat chills. The flavonoids have anti-inflammatory properties that reduce blood vessel constriction, improving blood flow to the skin and extremities. Terpene lactones, particularly ginkgolides, inhibit platelet-activating factor, which can cause blood vessel constriction and worsen chills. The antioxidants in ginkgo biloba also help to protect against oxidative stress, which can contribute to the onset of chills. By improving circulation and reducing oxidative stress, ginkgo biloba tinctures can help alleviate chills and promote overall warmth.
remedies.image_alt: Ginkgo biloba herbal tinctures for chills
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Ginkgo biloba leaves, a clean glass jar with a lid, and a dark glass bottle for storage.
Combine the Ginkgo biloba leaves and 2 cups of 80-proof vodka in the glass jar. Stir well.
Seal the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into the dark glass bottle. Discard the solids.
Label the bottle and store it in a cool, dark place. Take 20-30 drops in water or tea as needed to help relieve chills.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus tinctures contains several bioactive constituents, including eucalyptol, a natural decongestant that helps to relieve chills by reducing inflammation and congestion in the body. Eucalyptol also has a cooling effect, which can help to bring down a fever and make you feel more comfortable. Tannins and flavonoids in the tincture have anti-inflammatory properties that can help to ease chills by reducing the body's natural response to infection. The menthol in eucalyptus globulus tinctures also helps to open up airways and improve circulation, which can help to warm the body and alleviate chills. By combining these properties, eucalyptus globulus tinctures can provide effective relief from chills and fever.
remedies.image_alt: Eucalyptus globulus herbal tinctures for chills
remedy_procedure: Gather 250ml of 80% vodka and 100g of Eucalyptus globulus leaves.
Combine the vodka and Eucalyptus leaves in a clean glass jar, leaving 2cm of space at the top.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily.
After 2-3 weeks, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass jar.
Discard the solids and store the tincture in a dark glass bottle, using 5-7 drops as needed to relieve chills.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinchona officinalis tinctures contains the bioactive constituents quinine and alkaloids, which have antipyretic and anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds help to reduce fever and alleviate chills by decreasing the body's temperature and relieving the inflammation that often accompanies them. The antipyretic effect of quinine is particularly useful in treating malaria and other fevers that cause chills, while the anti-inflammatory properties help to soothe the body and promote recovery. The alkaloids present in Cinchona officinalis tinctures also have astringent properties, which help to dry up excess fluids and relieve chills caused by colds and flu. By combining these properties, Cinchona officinalis tinctures can help to effectively manage chills and promote a sense of well-being.
remedies.image_alt: Cinchona officinalis herbal tinctures for chills
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Cinchona officinalis bark, a glass jar with a lid, a cup of vodka or glycerin, and a cheesecloth.
Dry the Cinchona officinalis bark in a low-temperature oven (150°F) for 1 hour.
Combine the dried bark and vodka or glycerin in the glass jar. The jar should be about 1/4 full of the plant material.
Seal the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day.
Strain the mixture through the cheesecloth into another container. Discard the solids and store the tincture in a cool, dark place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Astragalus membranaceus tinctures contains a high concentration of bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, saponins, and polysaccharides. These compounds have immunomodulatory properties, which help regulate the body's immune response and prevent infections. The saponins in Astragalus membranaceus have anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce fever and alleviate chills by decreasing the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines. The polysaccharides in the tincture have been shown to stimulate the production of white blood cells, further enhancing the immune system's ability to fight off infections. By regulating the immune response and reducing inflammation, Astragalus membranaceus tinctures can help alleviate chills and fever caused by infection.
remedies.image_alt: Astragalus membranaceus herbal tinctures for chills
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Astragalus membranaceus roots and 2 cups of 80% ethanol (vodka or rubbing alcohol work too).
Combine the dried Astragalus roots and ethanol in a clean glass jar. Stir to coat all roots.
Seal the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day.
After 2-3 weeks, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into another clean glass jar. Discard the solids.
Store the tincture in a dark glass bottle with a dropper lid. Take 20-30 drops in water 2-3 times a day as needed for chills.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra tinctures contains glycyrrhizin, a triterpenoid saponin that has been used to treat various health issues. Glycyrrhizin has anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce fever and alleviate chills. The tincture also contains flavonoids and phenolic acids, such as liquiritin and isoliquiritin, which have been shown to have antiviral and antibacterial properties. These compounds work together to help fight off infection and reduce the symptoms of chills. The anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties of glycyrrhiza glabra tinctures make it a useful remedy for treating chills.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal tinctures for chills
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Glycyrrhiza glabra roots and 2 cups of 80% vodka.
Place the dried roots in a clean glass jar.
Pour the vodka over the roots, making sure they are completely covered.
Seal the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2 weeks, shaking it every day.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Panax quinquefolius tinctures contains ginsenosides, particularly Rg1, Re, Rb1, and Rb2, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antiviral effects, which help alleviate chills caused by infections. The tinctures also contain eleutherosides, which boost the immune system and help fight off pathogens. The antipyretic properties of Panax quinquefolius tinctures help reduce fever, making it an effective remedy for chills. The combination of these active constituents helps regulate body temperature and alleviate chills caused by infections.
remedies.image_alt: Panax quinquefolius herbal tinctures for chills
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Panax quinquefolius roots. You can find them in health food stores or online.
Combine the roots with 2 cups of 80-proof vodka in a clean glass jar. The jar should be big enough to hold all the ingredients with some space left.
Store the jar in a cool, dark place and shake it well every day for 2-3 weeks. This will help the roots release their goodness into the vodka.
After 2-3 weeks, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar. Discard the solids.
Transfer the tincture to dark glass dropper bottles and store them in a cool, dark place. Use 20-30 drops as needed to treat chills.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help prevent chills from forming. Certain herbs, like peppermint and ginger, have natural warming properties that can help increase body temperature. The caffeine in some herbal teas can also help stimulate blood circulation, which can help counteract the cold feeling. This can provide temporary relief from chills.
faq_safe: Drinking herbal teas for chills every day can be safe if you choose teas that are caffeine-free and made from natural ingredients. However, some teas can interact with medications or worsen health conditions. Be aware of the ingredients and potential effects, and consume them in moderation.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas can help with chills, but it depends on the tea and individual. Some teas, like peppermint or ginger, can start working within 15-30 minutes, providing relief from chills and discomfort. Others might take longer, up to an hour or more, to show noticeable effects.
faq_time: For chills, it's best to drink herbal tea in the evening. As your body temperature drops at night, the tea can help warm you up from the inside out. The calming effects of herbal tea can also help you relax and get a good night's sleep, which can aid in recovering from a chill.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas can interact with prescription medications for chills if they contain ingredients that affect blood pressure, heart rate, or liver function. For example, if you're taking a medication for a fever and you drink a tea with a stimulant like ginseng, it could increase your heart rate. Always be aware of the ingredients in your herbal teas.
faq_pregnant: When you're pregnant and feeling chilly, some herbal teas can be tempting. However, not all of them are safe. For example, peppermint and sage teas are usually okay in moderation. But teas like raspberry leaf and blue cohosh can cause problems, especially if you're close to your due date.
faq_breastfeeding: Herbal teas for chills while breastfeeding can be a good choice, but be careful what you choose. Some herbs, like peppermint and chamomile, are considered safe. However, others, like sage and pennyroyal, may not be suitable. Always check the ingredients and look for products labeled as safe for breastfeeding mothers.
title_2: 8 Herbal Tinctures to Ease Chills in Infants
